classes.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry

methods.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry.create_reform
documentation:
Generate an OpenFisca reform from PolicyEngine parameters.

Args:
    parameters (dict): The PolicyEngine parameters.

Returns:
    Reform: The OpenFisca reform.

methods.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry.create_microsimulations
documentation:
Generate a microsimulations from PolicyEngine parameters.

Args:
    parameters (dict): The PolicyEngine parameters.
    force_refresh_baseline (bool): If True, force a refresh of the baseline microsimulation.
    do_not_cache (bool): If True, do not cache the microsimulation.

methods.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry.create_individualsims
documentation:
Generate a individual simulations from PolicyEngine parameters.

Args:
    parameters (dict): The PolicyEngine parameters.
    situation (dict): The OpenFisca situation JSON.

methods.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry.create_openfisca_simulation
documentation:
Initialises an OpenFisca simulation from given household or reform parameters (optimised for performance by skipping no-reform applications).

Args:
    parameters (dict): Policy reform parameters, and a 'household' entry.

Returns:
    Simulation: The OpenFisca Simulation object.

class_variables.name: policyengine.country.country.PolicyEngineCountry.api_endpoints

documentation: The name of the country.

Base class for a PolicyEngine country. Each country has a set of API endpoints available.

classes.name: policyengine.country.results_config.PolicyEngineResultsConfig
documentation: Configuration class for calculating and displaying policy impacts using an OpenFisca country model.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.parameters.get_enum_map_from_variable_name
documentation:
Get the mapping of enum values to their labels from a variable name.

Args:
    variable (str): The name of the variable.
    variables (Dict[str, Variable]): The variables in the tax benefit system.

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: The mapping of enum values to their labels.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.parameters.flow_breakdown_parameter_metadata_down
documentation:
Recursively add metadata to parameters that are part of a flow breakdown.

Args:
    parameters (ParameterNode): The parameters to add metadata to.
    variables (List[Variable]): The variables to use to determine the metadata.

Returns:
    ParameterNode: The parameters with metadata added.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.parameters.build_parameters
documentation:
Extracts PolicyEngine parameters from OpenFisca parameter metadata.

Args:
    system (TaxBenefitSystem): The tax-benefit system to extract from.
    date (str): The date to extract parameters for. Defaults to the current date.

Returns:
    Dict[str, dict]: The parameter metadata.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.parameters.PolicyEngineParameter.to_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary representation of the parameter.

Raises:
    ValueError: If a required property is not found. This will likely be due to a change in the OpenFisca API.

Returns:
    dict: The parameter metadata.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.variables.build_variables
documentation:
Extracts PolicyEngine parameters from OpenFisca parameter metadata.

Args:
    system (TaxBenefitSystem): The tax-benefit system to extract from.

Returns:
    Dict[str, dict]: The parameter metadata.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.variables.PolicyEngineVariable.to_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary representation of the variable.

Raises:
    ValueError: If a required property is not found. This will likely be due to a change in the OpenFisca API.

Returns:
    dict: The variable metadata.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.add_parameter_file
documentation:
Generates a reform adding a parameter file to the tree.

Args:
    path (str): The path to the parameter YAML file.

Returns:
    Reform: The Reform adding the parameters.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.structural
documentation:
Generates a structural reform.

Args:
    variable (Type[Variable]): The class definition of a variable to replace.

Returns:
    Reform: The reform object.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.parametric
documentation:
Generates a parametric reform.

Args:
    parameter (str): The name of the parameter, e.g. tax.income_tax.rate.
    value (float): The value to set as the parameter value.
    period (str): The time period to set it for. Defaults to a ten-year period from 2015 to 2025.

Returns:
    Reform: The reform object.

functions.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.apply_reform
documentation:
Applies a reform to a system.

Args:
    reform (tuple): The reform to apply.
    system (TaxBenefitSystem): The system to apply it to.

Returns:
    TaxBenefitSystem: The system with the reform applied.

classes.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.PolicyReform

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.PolicyReform._sanitise_parameters
documentation: Sanitises the parameters to ensure they are in the correct format.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.PolicyReform.edits_baseline
documentation: Returns whether the reform edits the baseline policy.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.PolicyReform.baseline
documentation: Returns the baseline policy.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.PolicyReform.reform
documentation: Returns the reform policy.
A PolicyReform is a complete specification of two policies: a baseline and a reformed policy.

methods.name: policyengine.country.openfisca.reforms.Policy.apply
documentation:
Applies the policy to a system. This essentially does the same as any `Reform.apply` method.

Args:
    system (TaxBenefitSystem): The system to apply it to.

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.by_provision.get_breakdown_and_chart_per_provision
documentation:
Generates a breakdown data structure with spending per provision.

Args:
    country (PolicyEngineCountry): Country object.
    parameters (dict): Parameters for the reform.
    baseline (Microsimulation): The baseline microsimulation.
    reformed (Microsimulation): The reformed microsimulation.
    config (PolicyEngineResultsConfig): Country configuration.

Returns:
    dict: The breakdown details.

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.age.age_chart
documentation:
Generates a bar chart showing the impact of a reform by age.

Args:
    baseline (Microsimulation): The baseline simulation.
    reformed (Microsimulation): The reformed simulation.
    config (Type[PolicyEngineResultsConfig]): The country metadata.

Returns:
    dict: The Plotly JSON.

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.budgetary_impact.bar_data
documentation:
Generates a pair of data points for a waterfall bar.

```
param start

Starting value of bar.

type start

float

param amount

Amount of bar.

type amount

float

return

DataFrame with two rows:
- In the case of start and end (start + amount) being on the same side
of zero, it will be one for the hidden white bar and one for the
true value.
- In the case of start and end being on opposite sides of zero, it will
be one for the positive value and one for the negative value.
Each row contains columns for value and color (which are specific to
the row), and label and amount (which are the same for both rows).

rtype

pd.DataFrame
```

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.budgetary_impact.waterfall_data
documentation:
Generates data for waterfall charts.

```
param amounts

List of amounts.

type amounts

list

param labels

List of labels corresponding to each amount.

type labels

list

return

DataFrame with two rows for each amount plus the total.

rtype

pd.DataFrame
```

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.budgetary_impact.tax_benefit_waterfall_data
documentation:
Generates data for tax benefit waterfall charts.

```
param baseline

Baseline microsimulation.

type baseline

Union[Microsimulation, IndividualSim]

param reformed

Reformed microsimulation.

type reformed

Union[Microsimulation, IndividualSim]

return

DataFrame with two rows for each component plus the total.

pd.DataFrame
```

```
Name of the component, e.g. "Tax revenues".

str

Name of the component, e.g. "Tax revenues".

float

Whether the component is population- vs. household-level.

bool
```

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.decile.individual_decile_chart

Chart of average or relative net effect of a reform by income decile.

```
DataFrame with columns for Decile, Relative change, and Average change.

"Relative change" or "Average change".
```

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.charts.poverty.poverty_chart
documentation:
Chart of poverty impact by age group and overall.

```
param baseline

Baseline microsimulation.

type baseline

Microsimulation

param reformed

Reform microsimulation.

type reformed

Microsimulation

return

JSON representation of Plotly chart with poverty impact for:
- Children (under 18)
- Working age adults (18 to State Pension age)
- Pensioners (State Pension age and above)
- Overall

rtype

dict
```

modules.name: policyengine.impact.population.metrics
documentation: Functions generating aggregates and other numerical outputs from microsimulation results.

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.metrics.headline_metrics
documentation:
Compute headline society-wide metrics.

```
param baseline

Baseline simulation.

type baseline

Microsimulation

param reformed

Reform simulation.

type reformed

Microsimulation

return

Dictionary with net_cost, poverty_change, winner_share,
loser_share, and gini_change.

rtype

dict
```

functions.name: policyengine.impact.population.metrics.spending

```
Baseline microsimulation.

type baseline

Microsimulation

param reformed

Reform microsimulation.

type reformed

Microsimulation

return

Reform net income minus baseline net income.

rtype

float
```

```
Number.

int

str
```

functions.name: policyengine.web_server.cache.add_params_and_caching
documentation:
Adds request parameters to a function call under the variable `params` and caches the result if the caching decorator is present.

Args:

    cache (PolicyEngineCache): The cache to lookup from and store results to.
    logger (PolicyEngineLogger): The logger to use to log timings.

Returns:
    Callable: The function with the Flask handling added.
